GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Optus is committed to keeping Australia connected", "That's why they're offering millions of their mobile customers additional data.", "It feels like everything is changing so quickly, right now. The way we live, the way we work, the way we keep in touch with those we care about the most. But Optus is offering hope during uncertain times, by helping Aussies stay connected – even while we're apart. The brand is proving just what kind of 'yes' people they are. They're encouraging responsible connections – the kind that take place over the internet, not at a bar (or a barre class, for that matter) – during this difficult time when you can no longer make physical ones. What does that mean for you? Well, data – and lots of it. Optus is offering more data during lockdown, to ensure you're never really alone, even while you're at home, staying solo. So the good news is, you'll now have more data to use on Netflix binges. Throughout April 2020 they're offering eligible customers an extra 20GB postpaid mobile data for 30 days, or 10GB data if you're on an eligible prepaid service and recharge with $40 or more. Plus, Optus is automatically bumping eligible Home Broadband plans to an unlimited data allowance until 31 May 2020 – with no complicated activation process required. Whether you spend it on Zoom calls with workmates, or binging every episode of Big Little Lies. We know which one we'd choose. Just like you, Optus is also busy adapting to the new normal. They're hard at work strengthening networks, so that they're better equipped to handle the influx of demand over the coming days. They also have extra financial hardship services, which means they'll be waiving late fees and ceasing disconnections until 30 April 2020, to better support those who are struggling the most, at the moment. Because let's face it – we could all use one less thing to worry about. We get it. Things are tough for so many people right now. But that's why, even during these difficult times, Optus is taking the lead to make things a little bit easier and ensure we can all stay connected – even when we're apart."
